notes: Descent system malfunctioned after entry at too steep an angle; first human-made object to reach the martian surface. Coordinates approximate.
First soft landing on Mars; transmitted about 20 seconds of data (a featureless partial image) before contact was lost, likely due to a global dust storm. Coordinates approximate.
Returned the first in-situ atmospheric profile data during descent; transmissions ceased at or just before landing. Coordinates approximate.
Lander separated early due to an electronics fault and flew past Mars by about 1300 km; no landing occurred.
First fully successful Mars landing; operated until November 1982. Ran the first life-detection experiments on Mars.
Operated until April 1980; observed water frost on the surface.
First airbag landing and first rover (Sojourner) on Mars; operated about 3 months.

region: Ultimi Scopuli, south polar layered terrain (approx.)
notes: Contact lost during descent; premature engine shutdown (spurious touchdown signal) is the presumed cause. Coordinates approximate.
Two impactor microprobes carried by Mars Polar Lander; never heard from after atmospheric entry. Coordinates approximate.
Operated over 6 years (planned 90 days); climbed Husband Hill; became embedded at 'Troy' in 2009; last contact March 2010.
Drove a record 45.16 km over 14+ years; mission ended after the June 2018 global dust storm.
First landing in the martian arctic; confirmed shallow subsurface water ice and detected perchlorates; operated ~5 months.
First sky-crane landing; found ancient habitable lake environments; still operating (climbing Mount Sharp).
First geophysics station on Mars; detected over 1300 marsquakes; mission ended December 2022 as dust reduced solar power.
Caching samples for Mars Sample Return; Ingenuity made 72 flights (first powered flight on another planet, 2021-2024); Perseverance still operating on the crater rim.
Landed successfully but two solar panels failed to deploy, blocking the antenna; located intact in MRO images in 2015.
Entry-descent demonstrator; a sensor fault caused premature thruster shutdown and free fall from ~3.7 km.

Landed 2021-05-14 23:18 UTC = 2021-05-15 07:18 Beijing time (Chinese sources cite May 15). First non-US fully successful Mars landing; Zhurong drove ~1.9 km before entering hibernation in May 2022, from which it did not wake.
